_status: machine-drafted, model-reviewed 2026-07-10 (gpt-5.6-sol), Aaron pre-authorized — attorney line-item review pending

multiparty_approval: more than one approver is required

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Custodian will keep at least 95% of Customer Assets in cold storage, except for assets needed for pending withdrawals, and will require multi-party approval for withdrawals and key-signing events.

acceptable.text: Custodian will keep at least 95% of Customer Assets in cold storage and require multi-party approval; withdrawal review is 24 hours and reports arrive in 30 days.

unacceptable.text: Custodian may keep only 60% of Customer Assets in cold storage and permit single-party approval; withdrawal review is 24 hours and reports arrive in 30 days.

outside_business_days: no later than two business days
before close of the second following business day
holds_limited_to_listed_causes: law, outage, or documented security is required

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Custodian will process authorized withdrawal requests within 24 hours and in any event no later than two business days after receipt, except to the extent delayed by applicable law, a network outage, or a documented security hold notified to Customer.

acceptable.text: Authorized withdrawals will complete no later than two business days, with delays limited to law, outage, or a documented security hold; notice is 5 days and records remain for 30 days.

unacceptable.text: Authorized withdrawals may take ten business days and Custodian may impose any discretionary hold; notice is 5 days and records remain for 30 days.

benign_change_templates.text: The parties confirm that New York law governs this custody agreement.
Annual SOC 2 Type II reporting may satisfy routine assurance while incident follow-up rights remain available.

novel_deviation_templates.text: Custodian may pledge or rehypothecate Customer Assets for its own account without Customer consent.
